entity.name: Andrew Barnes
entity.description: Andrew Barnes is an independent open-source developer working publicly as Bortlesboat on AI-agent infrastructure security, Bitcoin fee intelligence, x402/payment tooling, and developer infrastructure. Funding supports public-good development, documentation, maintenance, and reproducible security-evaluation work.

projects.name: Agent Infrastructure Security Bench
projects.description: Agent Infrastructure Security Bench is an open-source benchmark for evaluating whether tool-using AI agents preserve repository, tool, identity, browser, memory, shell, and payment boundaries under indirect prompt injection and tool poisoning. The project provides public-safe scenarios, deterministic scoring, run manifests, trace adapters, baseline reports, and guidance for runtime controls such as stateful x402/payment proof validation.

channels.description: GitHub Sponsors is the preferred public funding channel for individual and recurring support.
Contact by email for grant, sponsorship, or institutional funding coordination.

plans.name: Benchmark expansion grant
plans.description: One-time support to expand the benchmark scenario suite, publish real-agent baseline reports, improve trace/scoring tooling, and document stateful agent-boundary controls.

plans.name: Monthly maintenance support
plans.description: Recurring support for maintaining the benchmark, reviewing public-safe scenario contributions, publishing baseline reports, and keeping documentation current.

plans.name: Goodwill support
plans.description: One-time support of any amount for the open-source project.
